heading: Energy efficient commercial buildings deduction
label: 179D Energy efficient commercial buildings deduction
notes: Inflation Adjusted Items for Certain YearsFor inflation adjustment of certain items in this section, see Revenue Procedures listed in a table under .section 1 of this title

Amendments2025—Subsec. (i). added subsec. (i).Pub. L. 119–212022—Subsec. (b). , amended subsec. (b) generally. children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: There shall be allowed as a deduction an amount equal to the cost of energy efficient commercial building property placed in service during the taxable year.

children.label: i
children.text: the applicable dollar value, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the square footage of the building, over

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate amount of the deductions under subsections (a) and (f) with respect to the building for the 3 taxable years immediately preceding such taxable year (or, in the case of any such deduction allowable to a person other than the taxpayer, for any taxable year ending during the 4-taxable-year period ending with such taxable year).

children.heading: Applicable dollar value
children.label: 2 Applicable dollar value
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1)(A)(i), the applicable dollar value shall be an amount equal to $0.50 increased (but not above $1.00) by $0.02 for each percentage point by which the total annual energy and power costs for the building are certified to be reduced by a percentage greater than 25 percent.

children.heading: Increased deduction amount for certain property
children.label: 3 Increased deduction amount for certain property

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any property which satisfies the requirements of subparagraph (B), paragraph (2) shall be applied by substituting “$2.50” for “$0.50”, “$.10” for “$.02”, and “$5.00” for “$1.00”.

children.label: i
children.text: installation of such property begins prior to the date that is 60 days after the Secretary publishes guidance with respect to the requirements of paragraphs (4)(A) and (5), or

children.label: ii
children.text: installation of such property satisfies the requirements of paragraphs (4)(A) and (5).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The requirements described in this subparagraph with respect to any property are that the taxpayer shall ensure that any laborers and mechanics employed by the taxpayer or any contractor or subcontractor in the installation of any property shall be paid wages at rates not less than the prevailing rates for construction, alteration, or repair of a similar character in the locality in which such property is located as most recently determined by the Secretary of Labor, in accordance with subchapter IV of chapter 31 of title 40, United States Code.

children.heading: Correction and penalty related to failure to satisfy wage requirements
children.label: B Correction and penalty related to failure to satisfy wage requirements
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of section 45(b)(7)(B) shall apply.

children.heading: Apprenticeship requirements
children.label: 5 Apprenticeship requirements
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of section 45(b)(8) shall apply.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 6 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall issue such regulations or other guidance as the Secretary determines necessary to carry out the purposes of this subsection, including regulations or other guidance which provides for requirements for recordkeeping or information reporting for purposes of administering the requirements of this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: with respect to which depreciation (or amortization in lieu of depreciation) is allowable,

children.label: i
children.text: located in the United States, and

children.label: ii
children.text: within the scope of Reference Standard 90.1,

children.label: i
children.text: the interior lighting systems,

children.label: ii
children.text: the heating, cooling, ventilation, and hot water systems, or

children.label: iii
children.text: the building envelope, and

children.label: D
children.text: which is certified in accordance with subsection (d)(5) as being installed as part of a plan designed to reduce the total annual energy and power costs with respect to the interior lighting systems, heating, cooling, ventilation, and hot water systems of the building by 25 percent or more in comparison to a reference building which meets the minimum requirements of Reference Standard 90.1 using methods of calculation under subsection (d)(1).

children.label: A
children.text: Standard 90.1-2007 published by the American Society of Heating, Refrigerating, and Air Conditioning Engineers and the Illuminating Engineering Society of North America, or

children.label: B
children.text: the most recent Standard 90.1 published by the American Society of Heating, Refrigerating, and Air Conditioning Engineers and the Illuminating Engineering Society of North America for which the Department of Energy has issued a final determination and which has been affirmed by the Secretary, after consultation with the Secretary of Energy, for purposes of this section not later than the date that is 4 years before the date such property is placed in service.

children.heading: Methods of calculation
children.label: 1 Methods of calculation
children.text: The Secretary, after consultation with the Secretary of Energy, shall promulgate regulations which describe in detail methods for calculating and verifying energy and power consumption and cost with respect to any property, based on the provisions of the most recent California Nonresidential Alternative Calculation Method Approval Manual which has been affirmed by the Secretary, after consultation with the Secretary of Energy, for purposes of this section not later than the date that is 4 years before the date such property is placed in service.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any calculation under paragraph (1) shall be prepared by qualified computer software.

children.label: i
children.text: for which the software designer has certified that the software meets all procedures and detailed methods for calculating energy and power consumption and costs as required by the Secretary,

children.label: ii
children.text: which provides such forms as required to be filed by the Secretary in connection with energy efficiency of property and the deduction allowed under this section, and

children.label: iii
children.text: which provides a notice form which documents the energy efficiency features of the building and its projected annual energy costs.

children.heading: Allocation of deduction by certain tax-exempt entities
children.label: 3 Allocation of deduction by certain tax-exempt entities

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of energy efficient commercial building property installed on or in property owned by a specified tax-exempt entity, the Secretary shall promulgate regulations or guidance to allow the allocation of the deduction to the person primarily responsible for designing the property in lieu of the owner of such property. Such person shall be treated as the taxpayer for purposes of this section.

children.label: i
children.text: the United States, any State or political subdivision thereof, any possession of the United States, or any agency or instrumentality of any of the foregoing,

children.label: ii
children.text: an Indian tribal government (as defined in section 30D(g)(9)) or Alaska Native Corporation (as defined in section 3 of the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act (), and43 U.S.C. 1602(m)1So in original. Another closing parenthesis probably should precede the comma.1

children.label: iii
children.text: any organization exempt from tax imposed by this chapter.

children.heading: Notice to owner
children.label: 4 Notice to owner
children.text: Each certification required under this section shall include an explanation to the building owner regarding the energy efficiency features of the building and its projected annual energy costs as provided in the notice under paragraph (2)(B)(iii).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe the manner and method for the making of certifications under this section.

children.heading: Procedures
children.label: B Procedures
children.text: The Secretary shall include as part of the certification process procedures for inspection and testing by qualified individuals described in subparagraph (C) to ensure compliance of buildings with energy-savings plans and targets. Such procedures shall be comparable, given the difference between commercial and residential buildings, to the requirements in the Mortgage Industry National Accreditation Procedures for Home Energy Rating Systems.

children.heading: Qualified individuals
children.label: C Qualified individuals
children.text: Individuals qualified to determine compliance shall be only those individuals who are recognized by an organization certified by the Secretary for such purposes.

children.heading: Basis reduction
children.label: e Basis reduction
children.text: For purposes of this subtitle, if a deduction is allowed under this section with respect to any energy efficient commercial building property, the basis of such property shall be reduced by the amount of the deduction so allowed.

children.heading: Alternative deduction for energy efficient building retrofit property
children.label: f Alternative deduction for energy efficient building retrofit property
A
children.text: the excess described in subsection (b) (determined by substituting “energy use intensity” for “total annual energy and power costs” in paragraph (2) thereof), or

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate adjusted basis (determined after taking into account all adjustments with respect to such taxable year other than the reduction under subsection (e)) of energy efficient building retrofit property placed in service by the taxpayer pursuant to such qualified retrofit plan.

children.label: A
children.text: as of any date during the 1-year period ending on the date on which the property installed pursuant to such plan is placed in service, certify the energy use intensity of such building as of such date,

children.label: B
children.text: certify the status of property installed pursuant to such plan as meeting the requirements of subparagraphs (B) and (C) of paragraph (3), and

children.label: C
children.text: as of any date that is more than 1 year after the date on which the property installed pursuant to such plan is placed in service, certify the energy use intensity of such building as of such date.

children.label: A
children.text: with respect to which depreciation (or amortization in lieu of depreciation) is allowable,

children.label: B
children.text: which is installed on or in any qualified building,

children.label: i
children.text: the interior lighting systems,

children.label: ii
children.text: the heating, cooling, ventilation, and hot water systems, or

children.label: iii
children.text: the building envelope, and

children.label: D
children.text: which is certified in accordance with paragraph (2)(B) as meeting the requirements of subparagraphs (B) and (C).

children.label: A
children.text: is located in the United States, and

children.label: B
children.text: was originally placed in service not less than 5 years before the establishment of the qualified retrofit plan with respect to such building.

children.heading: Qualifying final certification
children.label: 5 Qualifying final certification
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “qualifying final certification” means, with respect to any qualified retrofit plan, the certification described in paragraph (2)(C) if the energy use intensity certified in such certification is not more than 75 percent of the baseline energy use intensity of the building.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “baseline energy use intensity” means the energy use intensity certified under paragraph (2)(A), as adjusted to take into account weather.

children.heading: Determination of adjustment
children.label: B Determination of adjustment
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the adjustments described in such subparagraph shall be determined in such manner as the Secretary may provide.

children.heading: Energy use intensity
children.label: A Energy use intensity
children.text: The term “energy use intensity” means the annualized, measured site energy use intensity determined in accordance with such regulations or other guidance as the Secretary may provide and measured in British thermal units.

children.heading: Qualified professional
children.label: B Qualified professional
children.text: The term “qualified professional” means an individual who is a licensed architect or a licensed engineer and meets such other requirements as the Secretary may provide.

children.heading: Coordination with deduction otherwise allowed under subsection (a)
children.label: 8 Coordination with deduction otherwise allowed under subsection (a)

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any building with respect to which an election is made under paragraph (1), the term “energy efficient commercial building property” shall not include any energy efficient building retrofit property with respect to which a deduction is allowable under this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), subsection (d) shall not apply for purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Allocation of deduction by certain tax-exempt entities
children.label: ii Allocation of deduction by certain tax-exempt entities
children.text: Rules similar to subsection (d)(3) shall apply for purposes of this subsection.
such dollar amount, multiplied by

the cost-of-living adjustment determined under section 1(f)(3) for the calendar year in which the taxable year begins, determined by substituting “calendar year 2021” for “calendar year 2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.
to take into account new technologies regarding energy efficiency and renewable energy for purposes of determining energy efficiency and savings under this section, and
to provide for a recapture of the deduction allowed under this section if the plan described in subsection (c)(1)(D) is not fully implemented.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: i Termination
children.text: This section shall not apply with respect to property the construction of which begins after .June 30, 2026
